pi_items.source: Library of Congress catalog - James Madison's Notes are cataloged as manuscripts in the Subseries 5E collection

pi_items.metadata_value: Notes of Debates in the Federal Convention of 1787
pi_items.verified_value: Notes of Debates in the Federal Convention of 1787
pi_items.source: Library of Congress official catalog and digital collections; confirmed via multiple LC records at https://www.loc.gov/item/mss31021x001
Library of Congress digital collections at https://www.loc.gov/collections/james-madison-papers/

pi_items.verified_value: James Madison Papers, Series 5, Subseries 5E (Madison's Original Notes on Debates in the Federal Constitutional Convention, 1787)
pi_items.source: Library of Congress catalog shows Notes on Debates are in Series 5, Subseries 5e at https://www.loc.gov/resource/mss31021a.01x01/ and https://www.loc.gov/item/mss31021x001. Series 1 is General Correspondence, 1723-1859, which does not contain the Notes of Debates.

Library of Congress catalog - James Madison Papers collection; confirmed via multiple LC sources including https://www.loc.gov/collections/james-madison-papers/articles-and-essays/james-madison-and-the-federal-constitutional-convention-of-1787/

Constitutional Convention of 1787 was held in Philadelphia from May 25 to September 17, 1787, in Independence Hall (Pennsylvania State House), as documented at https://philadelphiaencyclopedia.org/essays/constitutional-convention/ and other historical records

pi_items.verified_value: Autograph manuscript (written in Madison's own hand)
pi_items.source: Library of Congress records confirm Madison wrote the notes in his own hand during and after each day of the convention sessions, as documented in multiple LC sources

privileged_info_summary: James Madison's 'Notes of Debates in the Federal Convention of 1787' is a genuine autograph manuscript held at the Library of Congress, Manuscript Division. The author is James Madison, the title is correct, and the convention took place in Philadelphia in 1787. The manuscript is cataloged as James Madison Papers, Series 5, Subseries 5E (not Series 1, Box 1 as claimed in the task metadata). The notes are in the public domain and available digitally through the Library of Congress.
